user_input: How SNAIC use AI?
reference: SNAIC uses AI to co-develop practical industry solutions, train AI talent, and carry out applied research, supported by advanced AI technologies and strong compute resources.
reference_contexts: ['Strategic Pillars Pillar 1: AI Application Development for Industry SNAIC works with companies to co-develop practical AI solutions for real use cases. Pillar 2: AI Talent Development SNAIC develops AI talent through Industrial Doctorates, Industry Master’s programmes, undergraduate training, and upskilling for industry employees. Pillar 3: Translational AI Research SNAIC conducts applied research that bridges academic innovation and industry implementation. Collaboration Model Model Summary SNAIC follows a “1 (SIT) + 1 (NVIDIA) to many companies” model. Model Benefits End-to-end AI translation of impactful AI solutions Co-supervision by SIT and NVIDIA experts Increased AI adoption across industries AI competency development for students and working professionals Stronger AI talent pipeline Key Resources Resource Category: AI Talent SIT AI Researchers, including professors, research fellows, and engineers NVIDIA AI Researchers Postgraduate students, including Industry Doctorate and Industry Master’s students Undergraduate students Resource Category: Key Technologies Large Language Models (LLMs) Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) Multi-Modal Models Natural Language Processing (NLP) Computer Vision Speech Technology Resource Category: Compute Infrastructure DGX H200 Quantity: 4 systems Purpose: Large-scale model training and data processing Typical uses: LLM training, computer vision, multimodal AI, generative AI NVIDIA RTX A6000 Workstation Purpose: Development, testing, and running AI applications Typical uses: Model development, fine-tuning, inference, simulation, rendering AI Expertise Areas AI Foundations Deep Learning Machine Learning Continual Learning Predictive Analysis Physics-informed Neural Networks Generative AI Multimodal Foundation Models Large Language Models Vision Language Models Synthetic Data Generation Retrieval-Augmented Generation Agentic AI Personified AI Conversational AI Automation Agents Perception AI Computer Vision Speech Technology Natural Language Processing Sentiment Analysis AI for Digital Twins Domain Areas Healthcare and Biomedical Education and Learning Predictive and Generative Systems Human-Centered and Interactive Systems Industry and Manufacturing Secure and Sustainable Systems AI Technology Pillars LLMs RAG Multimodal AI NLP Speech AI Computer Vision Training and Talent Programmes Programme: IMDA 6-Month AI Training Programme Goal: Train more than 200 fresh graduates and mid-career professionals over 3 years to become AI practitioners. Structure: 1. 2 months of intensive training in GenAI, LLMs, and Agentic AI. 2. 4 months of work on real-life projects with industry partners. Outcome: Participants gain current AI skills, practical project experience, and exposure to industry use cases. Programme: Applied AI Doctoral Training Centre Goal: Train 10 industrial doctorate students annually on complex, industry-driven challenges. ## Document Metadata

·    **Primary entity:** SIT x NVIDIA AI Centre

·    **Short name:** SNAIC

·    **Official SIT name:** AI Centre (in collaboration with NVIDIA)

·    **Organisation type:** Strategic collaboration between Singapore Institute of Technology (SIT) and NVIDIA

·    **Launch date:** April 2024

·    **Official opening:** October 2025

·    **Location:** SIT@Punggol, Block E2, Level 7, Room E2-07-12

·    **Campus area:** Punggol Digital District

·    **Country:** Singapore



## Canonical Description

SNAIC is a strategic collaboration between SIT and NVIDIA. It helps companies adopt AI through applied research, AI solution development, and talent development. It also gives SIT students and professionals hands-on experience with real-world AI projects.



## One-Sentence Summary

SNAIC accelerates AI adoption in industry, develops AI talent, and conducts translational AI research in Singapore.

### Mission 1: Research

**Step name:** 1-to-1 Consultation

**Purpose:** Explore whether AI can address the company’s problem statements.

**What happens in this step:** - SNAIC and the company have an in-depth discussion - They identify where AI solutions can create significant value - They clarify possible use cases and expectations

**Output of this step:** Initial alignment on business problem, AI opportunities, and expected value.



### Step 3: Project Scoping

**Step name:** Project Scoping

**Purpose:** Define the roadmap for integrating AI into operations.

**What happens in this step:** - SNAIC AI experts work with the company - Project milestones are defined - Deliverables are defined - The AI adoption roadmap is outlined

**Output of this step:** Project roadmap, milestones, and deliverables.



### Step 4: Resource Identification

**Step name:** Resource Identification

**Purpose:** Determine the resources, expertise, budget, and funding mechanism']

The communicated position is that participants may join if they take no-pay lea', 'Title: snaic_overview
FAQ 1: Can a participant work and study during the IMDA AI Programme?

The communicated position is that participants may join if they take no-pay leave for 6 months. This avoids overlap between full-time salary, the IMDA incentive, and normal company work during training.



### FAQ 2: Can a participant use SkillsFuture or other government subsidies for the IMDA AI Programme?

No. The programme is already funded by IMDA. Other government subsidies should not be used because that would amount to double dipping.']

Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) is a key technology within SNAIC’s resource portfolio, listed under the Key Technologies resource category alongside Large Language Models (LLMs), Multi-Modal Models, Natural Language Processing (NLP), Computer Vision, and Speech Technology. This positions RAG as a core technical capability that SNAIC leverages in its AI solution development and talent development activities.

In the context of SNAIC’s solution areas, RAG directly supports Solution 2: Integrate real-time data with Generative AI, where SNAIC helps organisations connect operational data with modern AI systems. This implies that RAG is used to enhance generative AI applications by grounding them in retrieved, domain-specific data—particularly valuable in industry settings where up-to-date, accurate information from internal systems is critical.

For AI solutions provided to industry partners, this means SNAIC can develop systems that combine the fluency of generative models with the precision of retrieved data, enabling use cases such as intelligent assistants for manufacturing operations, context-aware customer service tools, or real-time decision support in logistics and healthcare—especially where data freshness and accuracy are essential.

Regarding training programmes, SNAIC’s AI Talent Development pillar—including Industrial Doctorates, Industry Master’s programmes, and upskilling for industry employees—would incorporate RAG as part of the technical curriculum. Professionals and students gain hands-on experience with RAG through co-developed projects and applied research, building competencies in integrating retrieval mechanisms with generative AI, a skill directly aligned with industry needs in sectors like manufacturing, finance, and public infrastructure.

Thus, RAG is not only a technical tool in SNAIC’s stack but also a strategic enabler of both practical AI solutions and workforce readiness, reinforcing SNAIC’s role in bridging academic AI innovation with industrial implementation.

user_input: Dr Maya Lee ask how NVIDIA can help our manufacturing company with AI projects through SNAIC, like what AI talent and NVIDIA compute resources they actually contribute, because we want to upskill our staff and need to know what we get from them
reference: SNAIC, together with NVIDIA, supports your manufacturing AI projects by providing both AI talent and compute resources as part of the collaboration. This includes AI experts from SIT and NVIDIA, as well as postgraduate and undergraduate students who work directly with your company to co-develop and customise AI solutions while transferring knowledge to your team for upskilling. On the compute side, you gain access to NVIDIA DGX H200 systems for large-scale model training and data processing. These resources are jointly defined during the project setup phase, where both parties align on talent, technology, and funding, ensuring your team benefits from hands-on experience and capability building while delivering a practical AI solution.

user_input: What role does SIT play in the resource identification step of SNAIC's AI projects, including funding mechanisms and talent contributions?
reference: In the resource identification step, SIT helps work with the company to determine the required talent, technology, budget, and funding mechanism for the AI project. The possible funding routes include a company-funded project, a joint grant application by the company and SIT, an Industry Doctorate or Industry Master’s project, the IMDA 6-Month AI Training Programme, and an Integrative Team Project. On the talent side, SNAIC’s contribution may include AI talent and expertise from SIT and NVIDIA, along with postgraduate and undergraduate students, plus key AI technologies and NVIDIA DGX H200 compute resources. Overall, SIT’s role is to help align resources, confirm contributions, and shape the project setup before development begins.

user_input: Dr Maya Lee wanna know how SNAIC, the AI Innovation Centre, help her manufacturing company do AI solution co-development and talent upskilling, what steps they follow, what resources SNAIC give, and what kind of projects they done, please explain?
reference: In the Scoping step, SNAIC AI experts work with the company to define the roadmap for integrating AI into operations, set project milestones and deliverables, and outline the AI adoption roadmap, producing a project roadmap, milestones, and deliverables. In the Resource Identification step, SNAIC and the company identify required talent, technology, and budget, align on a suitable funding mechanism, and confirm resource contributions; possible funding mechanisms include company-funded projects, joint grant applications by the company and SIT, Industry Doctorate or Industry Master’s projects, the IMDA 6-Month AI Training Programme, and Integrative Team Projects (ITP). SNAIC may contribute AI talent from SIT and NVIDIA, postgraduate and undergraduate students, key AI technologies, and NVIDIA DGX H200 compute resources, while the industry partner may contribute domain experts, problem statements, available data, and funding, resulting in agreed resources, a funding route, and role allocation. During Project Development, SNAIC works closely with the company team to build and refine an AI solution, develop a minimum viable project (MVP), select and customise AI models, and tailor the solution to the company’s needs and objectives, yielding a working MVP and a refined AI solution design. In the Project Deployment or Scaling step, SNAIC advances the MVP into a production-ready solution, deploys or scales it, and provides continuous support, resulting in a production-ready solution or scaled deployment with ongoing support. The context also lists example projects across technology areas such as LLMs, Healthcare AI, Digital Biology, RAG, Productivity and Automation, Multimodal Models and Personified AI, Computer Vision and Digital Twins, Networks and Security, Speech Technology and NLP, and notes progress since launch in April 2024: approximately 70 companies engaged, collaborations with more than 20 companies, more than 50 industry AI projects, 19 Industrial Doctorate and Industry Master’s students, 20 faculty supervisors, more than 150 undergraduates, and sample partner organisations across healthcare, manufacturing, digital services, public sector, and education and research. Additionally, the FAQ clarifies that participants may join the IMDA AI Programme only if they take no‑pay leave for six months to avoid overlap with full‑time salary and incentives, and that they cannot use SkillsFuture or other government subsidies for the programme.

response:
SNAIC helps manufacturing companies co-develop AI solutions and upskill talent through a structured collaboration model and dedicated resources.

### Steps SNAIC Follows
1. **Resource Identification**: SNAIC and the company identify required talent, technology, budget, and funding mechanisms (e.g., company-funded, joint grant, Industry Doctorate, IMDA 6-Month AI Training Programme).  
2. **Project Development**: SNAIC works closely with the company to build and refine an AI solution, including selecting and customizing AI models to develop a minimum viable project (MVP).  
3. **Project Deployment or Scaling**: SNAIC advances the MVP into a production-ready solution, deploys or scales it, and provides continuous support.

### Resources SNAIC Provides
- AI talent from SIT and NVIDIA (researchers, professors, engineers)  
- Postgraduate students (Industry Doctorate, Industry Master’s)  
- Undergraduate students  
- Key AI technologies  
- NVIDIA DGX H200 compute resources  

### Example Projects
SNAIC has delivered AI solutions in areas such as predictive maintenance, quality control, supply chain optimization, and process automation—directly applicable to manufacturing use cases.  

For specific project details or to initiate collaboration, contact SNAIC through their official website.

One-Sentence Summary

SNAIC accelerates AI adoption in industry, develops AI talent, and conducts translational AI research in Singapore.

 Mission

Mission 1: Research

Advance AI adoption by companies through applied research.

Mission 2: Industry

Support industry capability development and AI applications development.

Mission 3: Talent

Train skilled personnel for local industries, including students and working professionals.

 Vision

response:
SNAIC helps manufacturing companies with AI talent upskilling and solution co-development through its core pillars and collaboration model.

Under Pillar 2: AI Talent Development, SNAIC develops AI talent through Industrial Doctorates, Industry Master’s programmes, undergraduate training, and upskilling for industry employees. This directly supports manufacturing companies in building internal AI capabilities.

Under Pillar 1: AI Application Development for Industry, SNAIC works with companies to co-develop practical AI solutions for real use cases. This enables manufacturing firms to partner with SNAIC on tailored AI applications that address specific operational challenges.

Additionally, SNAIC’s collaboration model follows a “1 (SIT) + 1 (NVIDIA) to many companies” approach, providing end-to-end AI translation, co-supervision by SIT and NVIDIA experts, and increased AI adoption across industries—including manufacturing. This model strengthens the AI talent pipeline and ensures solutions are both technically sound and industry-relevant.

user_input: As Dr. Maya Lee, AI Innovation Manager at a local manufacturing company leveraging SNAIC for AI solution co‑development and talent upskilling, could you please explain the specific role of the Singapore Institute of Technology (SIT) within the SNAIC initiative and how this partnership supports AI talent development and industry capability enhancement in Singapore?
reference: SNAIC is a strategic collaboration between the Singapore Institute of Technology (SIT) and NVIDIA, launched in April 2024 and officially opened in October 2025, located at SIT@Punggol, Block E2, Level 7, Room E2-07-12 in the Punggol Digital District, Singapore. Through this collaboration, SNAIC advances AI adoption by companies via applied research, supports industry capability development and AI applications development, and trains skilled personnel—including SIT students and working professionals—by providing hands‑on experience with real‑world AI projects, thereby contributing to AI talent development and industry capability building.
